components.name: Plan Comparison Card
components.description: Displays a single insurance plan with pricing and features for comparison views. Used in plan selection flows where users must choose between multiple options.

variants.name: Selected
variants.description: Highlighted state when user selects this plan

variants.name: Recommended
variants.description: Badge and accent border indicating the recommended option

properties.name: planName
properties.description: The name of the plan displayed as the card title

properties.name: priceRange
properties.description: Monthly price display string, e.g. $29/mo

properties.name: features
properties.description: Array of exactly 3 feature bullet points

properties.name: isSelected
properties.description: Whether the card is in the selected state

properties.name: isRecommended
properties.description: Whether to show the recommended badge and accent border

do: Use in plan selection flows where users choose between options
Always show exactly 3 features per card
Use the Recommended variant for the most popular or best-value plan
dont: Do not use for product cards outside of insurance plan selection
Do not show more than 4 cards in a row
Do not omit the price range — it is required

components.codeSnippet:
<PlanComparisonCard
  planName="Basic"
  priceRange="$29/mo"
  features={['Dental cover', 'Optical cover', 'Physio included']}
  isRecommended={true}
/>

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main call-to-action button. Use for the single most important action on a page or in a form. Only one primary button should appear in any given view.

variants.name: Disabled
variants.description: Inactive state — reduced opacity, not clickable

variants.name: Loading
variants.description: Spinner shown while async action is in progress

properties.name: label
properties.description: Button text label

properties.name: onClick
properties.description: Click handler function

properties.name: isDisabled
properties.description: Renders the disabled variant

properties.name: isLoading
properties.description: Shows spinner and suppresses click events

properties.name: fullWidth
properties.description: Stretches button to fill its container

do: Use for the primary action on any screen
Keep label text short — ideally 2–3 words
Use the Loading variant during async operations to prevent double submission
dont: Do not use more than one primary button in a single view
Do not use for destructive actions — use a danger variant instead
Do not use for navigation — use a link or secondary button

components.name: Form Field
components.description: A labelled input field with optional helper text and error state. The standard unit for all form inputs across the product.

variants.name: Error
variants.description: Validation failed — red border and error message shown

properties.name: label
properties.description: Field label displayed above the input

properties.name: value
properties.description: Current input value (controlled component)

properties.name: onChange
properties.description: Change handler

properties.name: placeholder
properties.description: Placeholder text shown when field is empty

properties.name: helperText
properties.description: Explanatory text shown below the input

properties.name: errorMessage
properties.description: Error text — triggers the error variant when present

properties.name: isDisabled
properties.description: Renders the disabled variant

do: Always include a visible label — never rely on placeholder text alone
Show the error variant with a clear message when validation fails
Use helper text to clarify format requirements before the user submits
dont: Do not disable a field without explaining why elsewhere on the page
Do not use placeholder text as a substitute for a label
Do not show error states before the user has interacted with the field

components.codeSnippet:
<FormField
  label="Date of birth"
  value={dob}
  onChange={setDob}
  placeholder="DD/MM/YYYY"
  helperText="We use this to calculate your premium"
  errorMessage={errors.dob}
/>

components.name: Alert Banner
components.description: A full-width banner for communicating system-level messages, warnings, or errors. Appears at the top of a page or section.

properties.name: variant
properties.description: One of: info, success, warning, error

properties.name: message
properties.description: The message text to display

properties.name: isDismissible
properties.description: Shows a close button allowing the user to dismiss the banner

properties.name: onDismiss
properties.description: Callback fired when the banner is dismissed

do: Use for page-level or system-level messages only
Keep message text concise — one or two sentences maximum
Allow dismissal for informational and success banners
dont: Do not use for inline field validation — use the Form Field error state instead
Do not stack multiple banners on a single page
Do not use the error variant for warnings — reserve it for blocking failures

components.codeSnippet:
<AlertBanner
  variant="success"
  message="Your application has been submitted successfully."
  isDismissible={true}
  onDismiss={() => setShowBanner(false)}
/>

components.name: Progress Stepper
components.description: A horizontal step indicator for multi-step flows such as applications, onboarding, and checkout. Shows the user where they are and how many steps remain.

variants.name: Step — Complete
variants.description: Step has been finished — shows checkmark

variants.name: Step — Active
variants.description: Current step — filled accent circle with step number

variants.name: Step — Upcoming
variants.description: Not yet reached — empty circle with step number

properties.name: steps
properties.description: Array of step label strings

properties.name: currentStep
properties.description: Index (0-based) of the currently active step

properties.name: onStepClick
properties.description: If provided, completed steps become clickable for back-navigation

do: Use for flows with 3–6 clearly distinct steps
Label each step with a short noun phrase — e.g. Your details, Review, Confirm
Allow back-navigation to completed steps where the flow permits
dont: Do not use for flows with fewer than 3 or more than 6 steps
Do not use step numbers as the only indicator — always include labels
Do not mark a step complete until all required fields within it are valid
